name: Testing diagnostics
description: Classify test failures and guide fixes for implementation, tests, or environment.
when_to_use: When the node runs tests, fixes failing tests, or configures test isolation.

testing.python.description: Python pytest, import path, conftest, fixtures, tmp_path, state isolation.
testing.python.when_to_use: Python/pytest tasks, import/collection errors, fixture or global state issues.
testing.python.prompt_fragment: Use pytest with conftest.py or pyproject.toml/pytest.ini for import paths. Prefer tmp_path/fixtures for isolation; reset module-level globals between tests. Do not weaken assertions to hide state leakage.

testing.java.description: JUnit, Maven/Gradle, Spring test profiles and test databases.
testing.java.when_to_use: Java/Spring tasks with JUnit or application-test.properties.
testing.java.prompt_fragment: Use JUnit with Maven/Gradle. For Spring, use a dedicated test profile and application-test.properties; prefer transactional rollback or embedded test DB over mutating production config.

testing.general.description: Oracle design, assertions, command policy, failure taxonomy.
testing.general.when_to_use: Assertion failures, wrong expected values, disallowed test commands, or cross-language rules.
testing.general.prompt_fragment: Classify failure: wrong oracle, broken test, environment/config, or implementation bug. Fix the right layer; keep tests meaningful. Respect test command allowlist and sandbox boundaries.
